title: Cirrus: Fix running Validate task on branches
body:
Similar to v3.0 branch PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9062

Followup to dbb9943

Despite skipping the `Smoke` check, it was observed on a *new* branch,
the `validate` task (specifically `git-validation`) will fail.  This
is because:

* `$CIRRUS_LAST_GREEN_CHANGE` will be empty on a new branch.
* `$CIRRUS_BASE_SHA` is always empty for runs triggered by branch-push
* `$EPOCH_TEST_COMMIT` will be set to `YOU_FOUND_A_BUG`.

Fix this by eliminating the `Smoke` task entirely, simplifying all
the `make validate` operations into the `validate` cirrus task.

Also, eliminate the `$CIRRUS_BUILD_ID` value as it's confusing and not
actually used anywhere.  It was formerly used for building VM images,
but this has moved to another repo entirely.

comments.body: @edsantiago hold off on responding please...I played around with simply eliminating "smoke" and it may be a simpler solution...
@edsantiago how about this now, I simplified it quite a bit, expanded comments, and confirmed (manually) that `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` works properly when you `source contrib/cirrus/lib.sh` on the VM.
Note to me: if this is acceptable, need to remove "Smoke" from the branch-protection list of required checks.
Note: Observed ~7min from code push to when `make validate` started running.  Previously this was <2 minutes (but smoke did not run all the validate checks).

New task-map, with `smoke` removed. 
![cirrus-map-pr9063](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/105773768-ab740900-5f21-11eb-89d6-752c30bba4ce.png)

Oh! Interesting, I'm glad I tested the branch-push code path.  [Somehow the VM doesn't know what 'origin/master' is](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6482953154854912):

```
$GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE/setup_environment.sh
fatal: ambiguous argument 'origin/master': unknown revision or path not in the working tree.
Use '--' to separate paths from revisions, like this:
'git <command> [<revision>...] -- [<file>...]'

Exit status: 128
```

@edsantiago do you understand what's going on here (when loading `lib.sh`)?  It must have something to with [how we populate the repository when running because of branch-push or tag-push.](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/62680c4ddc7e9e4c5db932a48f9878c9bea7527f/.cirrus.yml#L157-L169)
Ahh...wait a sec...this might simply be due to 'Check Automation' and 'Ext. services' use the default Cirrus-CI clone code, not our custom script...
...alright, let's try just moving the custom clone, so all jobs use it.
Ref: [Successful Cirrus-CI run w/ simulated branch-push variables](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5529167754166272)
Ready again for a peep (minor changes + rebase).

@cevich please LMK if/when this is ready for review again. 

If you need to re-push, could you please remove the `smoke` line from https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/efcd48b9668612eb975a299b79e7b6673a5addce/contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh#L184
Ah, looks like I was too late. OK, taking a new look.

> please remove the smoke line from

...and there might be more, I honestly didn't search for it very hard (i.e. at all).

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,saschagrunert] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/hold

@containers/podman-maintainers could someone PTAL at my question re: the changed `skip`?

I believe this is ready to merge, but I don't fully understand the branch/tag flows and would like a final blessing from someone who does.
/lgtm
/hold cancel

@cevich quick followup, please remember to:

>Note to me: if this is acceptable, need to remove "Smoke" from the branch-protection list of required checks.

> need to remove "Smoke" from the branch-protection list

Already done, thanks for the reminder though.
